H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H‑OS‑L1 (Pebble) duplicate entry included for completeness; see H‑OS‑L1 above.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Same as Pebble – helps many feel better after overstimulation, settling in about ten minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H‑OS‑L2 (Rainfall) duplicate entry; see H‑OS‑L2 above.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Same as Rainfall – calms sensory overload in roughly ten minutes.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H‑OS‑L3 (Glacier) duplicate entry; see H‑OS‑L3 above.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Same as Glacier – useful for winding down before sleep or work.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H‑AX‑L1 (Meadow) recorded n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong, with 63 % positive responses. The outcome distribution peaks at scores 7‑9 and contains few low scores. Qualitative data (n=23) shows predominantly self‑listeners (13) reporting moderate or noticeable improvement, with settlement typically around 5‑10 min and rhythm described as very settling. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views, no retention data), indicating high efficacy despite limited discovery. AutoGluon predicts an anxiety‑zone score of 7.84, modestly above the observed mean, suggesting the protocol may be slightly under‑utilized in routing.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners felt noticeably calmer after about ten minutes; it’s a reliable anxiety‑relief choice.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H‑AX‑L2 (Cottage) has n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful, and 67 % positive outcomes. The distribution is tighter than L1, with a mode around 7‑8. Qualitative respondents (n=20) are mostly self‑listeners; improvements are described as moderate or noticeable, and settle time centers on ~5 min. YouTube views are 6 with no retention metrics. AutoGluon does not rank this protocol as top for anxiety, indicating L1 may better match the zone.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Provides a quick anxiety lift, especially while driving, though some find the rhythm just okay.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H‑AX‑L3 (Valley) reports n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful, and 61 % positivity. The outcome histogram shows a slight bimodal tendency between 7 and 9‑10. Qualitative feedback (n=20) includes a higher proportion of caregiver listeners and a typical settle time of ~10 min or full session length. YouTube views are 4, retention unavailable. AutoGluon again favors L1 for anxiety, so L3 may be redundant for that zone.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Gentle and effective for caregivers, settling in around ten minutes.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H‑CR‑L1 (Anchor) presents n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, with 53 % positive scores. The distribution is capped at 9 and shows a bimodal cluster around 7‑8, reflecting a ceiling effect for acute listeners. Qualitative input (n=24) highlights significant improvement for 8 participants and rapid settlement (5‑10 min). YouTube reach is 6 views, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a higher crisis‑zone score (7.96) for H‑CR‑L2, suggesting potential benefit from adding a micro‑ascent tail to L1.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Offers fast relief in crisis moments, though not everyone reaches the highest calm level.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H‑CR‑L2 (Harbor) recorded n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, and 55 % positivity. Scores cluster around 6‑8 with modest spread. Qualitative respondents (n=16) report mixed music opinions but strong settling feedback (very calming). YouTube views=4, no retention. AutoGluon assigns the highest predicted crisis score (7.96) to this protocol, supporting its promotion over L1.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Gives moderate crisis support with a calm feel after roughly ten minutes.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H‑CR‑L3 (Horizon) has n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful, and 52 % positive outcomes. Distribution is relatively flat with immediate settlement for many users. Qualitative data (n=19) notes quick onset (within 2 min) but mixed music sentiment and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=5, no retention. AutoGluon does not flag this protocol for crisis routing, underscoring its lower predictive power.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Acts quickly for some during work or study, but reactions to the music vary.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H‑SL‑L1 (Lantern) has n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong, and 65 % positive scores. Distribution peaks at higher scores with a long tail. Qualitative respondents (n=15) are primarily self‑listeners, reporting rapid settlement (5‑10 min) and very settling rhythm. YouTube views are negligible (4). AutoGluon predicts a sleep‑zone score of 7.54, matching the observed mean, reinforcing its suitability for rest contexts.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Quickly promotes sleep or relaxation, with music most listeners love.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H‑SL‑L2 (Hammock) presents n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful, and 67 % positivity—the lowest std in the sleep sub‑category. Qualitative data (n=15) include many client/patient listeners, longer settle times (~10‑20 min), and mixed music opinions. YouTube views=5, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a slightly lower sleep score (≈7.2), suggesting comparable efficacy.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Works for sleep but settles a bit slower; some find the rhythm distracting.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H‑SL‑L3 (Midnight) recorded n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful, and 64 % positive outcomes. The outcome spread is moderate. Qualitative feedback (n=22) shows a mix of self‑ and support listeners, settlement around 5‑10 min, and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=3, no retention. AutoGluon aligns with observed sleep performance.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Calms within five to ten minutes; rhythm is mostly balanced but occasionally busy.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T‑FC‑L1 (Compass) presents n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful, and 56 % positivity. Distribution is broad with many mid‑range scores. Qualitative feedback (n=23) notes noticeable improvement but rhythm described as slightly busy, and settlement often immediate. YouTube views=5, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a higher focus score (7.73), indicating under‑performance relative to prediction.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Helps with focus quickly, but the rhythm can feel a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T‑FC‑L2 (Waypoint) has n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful, and 66 % positive outcomes. Scores cluster around 7‑9. Qualitative respondents (n=17) report significant improvement and a settle time around 10 min; rhythm is balanced. YouTube views=4, no retention. AutoGluon does not list this as top‑ranked, suggesting modest predictive advantage.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Provides clear thinking during tough tasks; music opinion is mixed.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T‑FC‑L3 (Summit) recorded n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful, and 71 % positive scores—the highest across THRIVING. Distribution is bimodal, with a notable cluster of 10s and another of mid‑range scores. Qualitative data (n=29) emphasize significant improvement and a settle time around 10 min; rhythm is balanced. YouTube views=15, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a focus score of 7.73, aligning with the high observed mean but highlighting the bimodal pattern as a research question.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Strongest focus boost, especially for chores or relaxing; many love the sound.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T‑CF‑L1 (Ember) has n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful, and 53 % positive scores. Distribution centers near 6‑8 with a modest tail. Qualitative input (n=21) reports slight improvement and immediate to 10‑min settlement; rhythm is described as balanced. YouTube views=2, no retention. AutoGluon does not highlight this protocol for creative flow, indicating room for improvement.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Gently supports creativity right away, though the rhythm feels slightly active.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T‑CF‑L2 (Current) shows n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful, and 58 % positivity. Scores are tighter, with many mid‑range ratings. Qualitative respondents (n=15) note significant improvement and a favored settle time of ~10 min; rhythm is balanced and music is loved. YouTube views=2, no retention. AutoGluon rates this as a modest performer.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Good for creative flow, especially while commuting or before sleep.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T‑CF‑L3 (Aurora) recorded n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing, and 67 % positive outcomes. The outcome distribution skews high, with many 8‑10 scores. Qualitative data (n=25) emphasize noticeable and significant improvement, balanced rhythm, and a settle time of 5‑10 min. YouTube views=7, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a modest boost (≈7.0), suggesting this protocol may be approaching its performance ceiling.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Boosts creative energy after about ten minutes; music is fine but not thrilling.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T‑MB‑L1 (Stone) shows n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful, and 64 % positivity. Distribution is tight around the mean. Qualitative feedback (n=23) includes therapy‑session usage and a long settle time (~20 min). YouTube views=3, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a maintenance score of 7.69, slightly above the observed mean.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Supports emotional balance in longer sessions, often used in therapy.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T‑MB‑L2 (Axis) records n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful, and 59 % positive outcomes. Scores are widely spread. Qualitative data (n=16) show balanced listener types, quick settlement (~5 min) for some, and rhythm described as balanced or slightly busy. YouTube views=4, no retention. AutoGluon’s maintenance prediction (7.69) exceeds the observed mean, indicating potential under‑utilization.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Aids steadiness at work or bedtime, though some hear a slightly busy rhythm.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T‑MB‑L3 (Orbit) has n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing, and 71 % positive scores despite four qualitative reports of non‑settlement. Qualitative respondents (n=19) note mixed settlement times and a generally liked rhythm. YouTube views=3, no retention. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance, suggesting further sampling is warranted.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Aims for balance but a few listeners didn’t feel it settle; still experimental.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X‑ME‑L1 (Breeze) shows n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful, and 54 % positive scores. Distribution leans toward mid‑range values. Qualitative data (n=22) highlight moderate to significant improvement, a long settle time (~20 min), and strong music love. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon predicts the highest library score (8.23) for low‑affect zones, indicating a strong cross‑bucket advantage.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Best for low‑energy moments, building slowly over twenty minutes; many love it.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X‑ME‑L2 (Sunrise) recorded n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful, and 64 % positivity. Scores are slightly higher than L1. Qualitative respondents (n=15) report rapid settle times (some <2 min) and high music love. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon’s low‑affect prediction (8.23) again exceeds observed mean, supporting its promotion.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Quick mood lift that many enjoy, especially when needing a fast pick‑up.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X‑ME‑L3 (Updraft) has n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing, and 56 % positive outcomes. Distribution is modestly left‑skewed. Qualitative feedback (n=25) includes moderate improvement for many, some reports of no effect, and a balanced rhythm. YouTube views=4, no retention. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, suggesting potential if design is refined.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed feelings; some notice a lift, others see no change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X‑MD‑L1 (Footpath) recorded n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful, and 45 % positive outcomes—the lowest positive rate in TRANSFORMING. Distribution is wide with many low scores. Qualitative data (n=17) indicate noticeable improvement for some but overall modest effect; rhythm is balanced. YouTube views=5, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a motivation score of 7.88, suggesting cross‑zone potential despite current under‑performance.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Provides a gentle motivation lift for about half the listeners.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X‑MD‑L2 (Trail) has n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing, and 43 % positivity. Scores are modestly low with a long settle time (~20 min). Qualitative respondents (n=21) report mixed improvement and a rhythm that is balanced or slightly busy. YouTube views=7, no retention. AutoGluon’s motivation prediction (7.88) again exceeds observed performance.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Supports drive with mixed reactions; some love it, others are indifferent.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X‑MD‑L3 (Switchback) presents n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing, and 62 % positive outcomes. Distribution is bimodal with clusters at 6 and 10. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is mixed; settlement is rapid for some, slower for others. YouTube views=7, no retention. AutoGluon does not prioritize this protocol, indicating the need for further data.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Variable energy boost; works for some, not for others—still early testing.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X‑MC‑L1 (Cavern) recorded n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful, and 54 % positivity. Distribution is modestly left‑skewed. Qualitative data (n=25) include a small subset (2) reporting worsening, while most note noticeable improvement; rhythm is balanced. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54, indicating room for improvement.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Improves mental clarity for many, though a few felt worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X‑MC‑L2 (Starlight) shows n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing, and 56 % positive outcomes. Scores cluster around 6‑8. Qualitative respondents (n=20) report significant improvement for many and a very settling rhythm. YouTube views=12, no retention. AutoGluon’s mind‑clarity prediction (7.54) exceeds the observed mean, suggesting a promising cross‑bucket signal.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Sharpens focus quickly; most listeners love the calming rhythm.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X‑MC‑L3 (Cosmos) presents n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing, and 50 % positivity—the lowest mean in the library. Distribution is bimodal with peaks near 5 and 8. Qualitative feedback (n=17) notes noticeable improvement for most, but rhythm is described as too busy by several. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54, a sizable gap that flags this protocol for redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results on clarity; some find it too busy and it scores low overall.

sphere_commentary.overview:
All three series show average outcomes between 6.5 and 7.2 with substantial individual variability, suggesting broad efficacy but diverse listener experiences.
• HEALING (n=574) has the largest sample and the lowest variance, indicating stable performance across its descent protocols.
• THRIVING (n=245) matches HEALING mean but displays the highest peak scores, especially in Focus & Clarity.
• TRANSFORMING (n=181) shows the lowest mean and the greatest spread, consistent with its ascending‑arc design.
• AutoGluon repeatedly flags X‑ME‑L1 as the top‑predicted protocol (score≈8.2) and highlights several cross‑bucket routing opportunities.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Efficacy patterns align with each series’ design intent while revealing useful cross‑series signals.
• HEALING’s descent protocols reliably regulate nervous system states; anxiety and overstimulation variants score above 7.0.
• THRIVING’s hold protocols build cognitive performance, with T‑FC‑L3 achieving the highest observed mean (7.63) and strong qualitative praise.
• TRANSFORMING’s mix of descent and ascent yields mixed outcomes; X‑ME‑L1 excels in low‑affect zones, whereas X‑MC‑L3 underperforms markedly.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A handful of protocols deviate from series‑wide expectations and merit closer scrutiny.
• X‑MC‑L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45) records the lowest mean and highest dispersion, hinting at a population mismatch.
• H‑CR‑L1 exhibits a bimodal distribution clustered at 7‑9 despite a modest mean, suggesting ceiling effects for acute listeners.
• T‑FC‑L3 shows a bimodal outcome (many 10s and many 6s) together with the widest spread in THRIVING, indicating possible subgroup effects.
• X‑MD‑L3 and H‑SL‑L1 display the greatest cross‑run drift (similarity <0.55), implying analytical instability that may reflect shifting data patterns.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements can address the flagged irregularities and leverage strong signals.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3: create an L4 variant with gentler descent slope to accommodate low‑affect tolerance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L1/L2/L3: add a brief micro‑ascent tail (final 60 s) to provide an accessible peak for acute listeners.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑ME‑L1: promote as primary routing for low‑affect, motivation, and mind‑clarity zones via Adviser.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3: investigate the bimodal outcome distribution and consider adjusting hold duration or phasing.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑MB‑L3: increase L3 sampling through targeted Adviser routing to validate its high positive rate despite settlement issues.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L1: explore cross‑zone routing to crisis and anxiety contexts, given AutoGluon’s high predicted motivation score (7.88).
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal rapid analytical evolution and coherent within‑series clustering.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 28 of 30 protocols show similarity <0.85 (notable drift), with X‑MD‑L3 (0.507) and H‑SL‑L1 (0.530) most unstable.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: strong intra‑series pairings (e.g., T‑FC‑L1↔L2 0.878, H‑SL‑L1↔L2 0.869) while L3 protocols (T‑FC‑L3, T‑CF‑L3, H‑OS‑L3) are analytically distinct.
• Confidence note: drift analysis spans ~2.5 months; clustering derives from the latest run, so both will stabilize as more data accrues.

Qualitative respondents (n=20) report significant improvement for many and a very settling rhythm. YouTube views=12, no retention. AutoGluon’s mind‑clarity prediction (7.54) exceeds the observed mean, suggesting a promising cross‑bucket signal.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X‑MC‑L3 (Cosmos) presents n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing, and 50 % positivity—the lowest mean in the library. Distribution is bimodal with peaks near 5 and 8. Qualitative feedback (n=17) notes noticeable improvement for most, but rhythm is described as too busy by several. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a mind‑clarity score of 7.54, a sizable gap that flags this protocol for redesign.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X‑MD‑L1 (Footpath) recorded n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful, and 45 % positive outcomes—the lowest positive rate in TRANSFORMING. Distribution is wide with many low scores. Qualitative data (n=17) indicate noticeable improvement for some but overall modest effect; rhythm is balanced. YouTube views=5, no retention. AutoGluon predicts a motivation score of 7.88, suggesting cross‑zone potential despite current under‑performance.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X‑MD‑L2 (Trail) has n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing, and 43 % positivity. Scores are modestly low with a long settle time (~20 min). Qualitative respondents (n=21) report mixed improvement and a rhythm that is balanced or slightly busy. YouTube views=7, no retention. AutoGluon’s motivation prediction (7.88) again exceeds observed performance.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X‑MD‑L3 (Switchback) presents n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing, and 62 % positive outcomes. Distribution is bimodal with clusters at 6 and 10. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is mixed; settlement is rapid for some, slower for others. YouTube views=7, no retention. AutoGluon does not prioritize this protocol, indicating the need for further data.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X‑ME‑L1 (Breeze) shows n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful, and 54 % positive scores. Distribution leans toward mid‑range values. Qualitative data (n=22) highlight moderate to significant improvement, a long settle time (~20 min), and strong music love. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon predicts the highest library score (8.23) for low‑affect zones, indicating a strong cross‑bucket advantage.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X‑ME‑L2 (Sunrise) recorded n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful, and 64 % positivity. Scores are slightly higher than L1. Qualitative respondents (n=15) report rapid settle times (some <2 min) and high music love. YouTube views=8, no retention. AutoGluon’s low‑affect prediction (8.23) again exceeds observed mean, supporting its promotion.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X‑ME‑L3 (Updraft) has n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing, and 56 % positive outcomes. Distribution is modestly left‑skewed. Qualitative feedback (n=25) includes moderate improvement for many, some reports of no effect, and a balanced rhythm. YouTube views=4, no retention. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, suggesting potential if design is refined.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners felt noticeably calmer after about ten minutes; it’s a reliable anxiety‑relief choice.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Provides a quick anxiety lift, especially while driving, though some find the rhythm just okay.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Gentle and effective for caregivers, settling in around ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Offers fast relief in crisis moments, though not everyone reaches the highest calm level.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Gives moderate crisis support with a calm feel after roughly ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Acts quickly for some during work or study, but reactions to the music vary.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Same as Pebble – helps many feel better after overstimulation, settling in about ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Same as Rainfall – calms sensory overload in roughly ten minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Same as Glacier – useful for winding down before sleep or work.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Quickly promotes sleep or relaxation, with music most listeners love.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Works for sleep but settles a bit slower; some find the rhythm distracting.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Calms within five to ten minutes; rhythm is mostly balanced but occasionally busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Gently supports creativity right away, though the rhythm feels slightly active.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Good for creative flow, especially while commuting or before sleep.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Boosts creative energy after about ten minutes; music is fine but not thrilling.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Helps with focus quickly, but the rhythm can feel a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Provides clear thinking during tough tasks; music opinion is mixed.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Strongest focus boost, especially for chores or relaxing; many love the sound.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Supports emotional balance in longer sessions, often used in therapy.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Aids steadiness at work or bedtime, though some hear a slightly busy rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Aims for balance but a few listeners didn’t feel it settle; still experimental.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Improves mental clarity for many, though a few felt worse.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Sharpens focus quickly; most listeners love the calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Mixed results on clarity; some find it too busy and it scores low overall.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Provides a gentle motivation lift for about half the listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Supports drive with mixed reactions; some love it, others are indifferent.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Variable energy boost; works for some, not for others—still early testing.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Best for low‑energy moments, building slowly over twenty minutes; many love it.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Quick mood lift that many enjoy, especially when needing a fast pick‑up.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Mixed feelings; some notice a lift, others see no change.
